- @param b Divisor; must not be zero.
- @return Quotient and remainder, or error if b is zero.

180: func Divide(a, b int) (int, int, error) {
181: 	if b == 0 {

183: 	}
184: 	return a / b, a % b, nil

### FUNCTION: `FanOut`
> Signature: `func FanOut(items []string, workers int) <-chan string`
> Lines: 221-243
- @param items Slice of strings to process.
- @param workers Number of concurrent worker goroutines.
- @return Channel delivering processed results.
```
221: func FanOut(items []string, workers int) <-chan string {
222: 	results := make(chan string, len(items))
223: 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
225: 	for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
226: 		wg.Add(1)
227: 		go func(id int) {
228: 			defer wg.Done()
230: 			for _, item := range items {
231: 				results <- fmt.Sprintf("[%d] %s", id, item)
232: 			}
233: 		}(i)
234: 	}
237: 	go func() {
238: 		wg.Wait()
239: 		close(results)
240: 	}()
242: 	return results
243: }
```

### FUNCTION: `Describe`
> Signature: `func Describe(v interface{}) string`
> Lines: 250-266
- @param v Any value to describe.
- @return Human-readable type description.
```
250: func Describe(v interface{}) string {
251: 	switch val := v.(type) {
252: 	case int:
253: 		return fmt.Sprintf("int: %d", val)
254: 	case string:
255: 		return fmt.Sprintf("string: %q", val)
256: 	case bool:
257: 		if val {
258: 			return "bool: true"
259: 		}
260: 		return "bool: false"
261: 	case nil:
262: 		return "nil"
263: 	default:
264: 		return fmt.Sprintf("unknown: %T", val)
265: 	}
266: }
```

### FUNCTION: `SafeExecute`
> Signature: `func SafeExecute(fn func()) (err error)`
> Lines: 273-282
- @param fn The function to execute safely.
- @return Error if fn panicked, nil otherwise.
```
273: func SafeExecute(fn func()) (err error) {
274: 	defer func() {
275: 		if r := recover(); r != nil {
277: 			err = fmt.Errorf("panic: %v", r)
278: 		}
279: 	}()
280: 	fn()
281: 	return nil
282: }
```
